"""Web scraper for judge/organizer biographical data and photos.

Uses Serper web search + image search to gather information,
then Claude Haiku to extract structured data from results.
"""

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os

import anthropic
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SERPER_SEARCH_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
SERPER_IMAGES_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/images"

# Full list of TreeHacks 2025 judges and organizers
JUDGES_LIST: list[dict] = [
    # Judges
    {"name": "Gautam Kumar", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Greg Feingold", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kyle Jeong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Shrey Pandya", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Diyi Zhu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sarah Josief", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Craig Dennis", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Judy Cheong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Rajesh Bhatia", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sabrina Farmin", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ben", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kelsea An", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Olivia Petrie", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sana Wajid", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Abhi Gangani", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dev Chauhan", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kshipra Dhame", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Prithvi Chaudhari", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ryan Tran", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jess Waterman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "David Yi", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Emma Kirst", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Claudia Dalmau", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Brandon", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Chad Rushing", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Daniel Sigman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Matt Luat", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Felicia Chang", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Connor Ling", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Maddie Bernheim", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Vincent Po", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Janelle Battad", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dj Isaac", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Max Forsey", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Zach Gulsby", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jeff Gardner", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Robin Lee", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sandy Orozco", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Hong Yi Chen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Lauren Goldberg", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Megan Ehrlich", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ojus Save", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Amanda Yiu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Advait Maybhate", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Martin Ceballos", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Marvin von Hagen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Michi Vinocour", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Samyok Nepal", "role": "judge"},
    # Organizers
    {"name": "Erin Shen", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Grace Wang", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Jerry Yao", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Madison Lea Ho", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Matthew Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Maxwell Spivakovsky", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Michael Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Oliver Sin", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Sahana Mantha", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Shrish Premkrishna", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Thryambak Ganapathy", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Toryn Thompson", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Tyler Rubenstein", "role": "organizer"},
]

# Names without a last name get extra context in the search query
_AMBIGUOUS_NAMES = {"Ben", "Brandon", "Dj Isaac"}


def _build_search_query(name: str, role: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper search query with appropriate context."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f'"{name}" TreeHacks 2025'
    role_label = "judge" if role == "judge" else "organizer"
    return f'"{name}" TreeHacks Stanford 2025 {role_label}'


def _build_image_query(name: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper image search query."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f"{name} TreeHacks headshot"
    return f"{name} headshot"


async def _serper_web_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper web search API and return organic results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_SEARCH_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("organic", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper web search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


async def _serper_image_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper image search API and return image results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_IMAGES_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("images", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper image search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


def _extract_with_llm(name: str, role: str, web_results: list[dict], image_results: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Use Claude Haiku to extract structured biographical data from search results.

    Returns a dict with: title, organization, bio, photo_url, linkedin_url,
    twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls.
    """
    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set"}

    # Format web results for the prompt
    web_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(web_results, 1):
        web_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Snippet: {r.get('snippet', '')}\n"
            f"   URL: {r.get('link', '')}\n\n"
        )

    # Format image results for the prompt
    image_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(image_results, 1):
        image_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Image URL: {r.get('imageUrl', '')}\n"
            f"   Source: {r.get('source', '')}\n\n"
        )

    prompt = (
        f"Extract biographical information about {name} (a TreeHacks 2025 {role}) "
        f"from these search results.\n\n"
        f"WEB RESULTS:\n{web_text}\n"
        f"IMAGE RESULTS:\n{image_text}\n"
        f"Return a JSON object with these exact keys:\n"
        f"- title: their job title or role (string or null)\n"
        f"- organization: their company or university (string or null)\n"
        f"- bio: a 2-3 sentence biography (string or null)\n"
        f"- photo_url: the best headshot URL from image results (string or null)\n"
        f"- linkedin_url: their LinkedIn profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- twitter_url: their Twitter/X profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- website_url: their personal website URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- confidence: how confident you are this data is correct - \"high\", \"medium\", or \"low\"\n"
        f"- source_urls: array of URLs where you found information\n\n"
        f"If the search results don't clearly match this person, set confidence to \"low\" "
        f"and leave fields as null. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
        message = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=512,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        )
        content = message.content[0].text
        return json.loads(content)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed for %s: %s", name, e)
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": str(e)}


async def scrape_single_judge(
    name: str,
    role: str,
    http_client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    api_key: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> dict:
    """Scrape biographical data for a single judge/organizer.

    Returns a dict with extracted data or an error.
    """
    async with semaphore:
        web_query = _build_search_query(name, role)
        image_query = _build_image_query(name)

        # Run web + image search in parallel
        web_results, image_results = await asyncio.gather(
            _serper_web_search(http_client, web_query, api_key),
            _serper_image_search(http_client, image_query, api_key),
        )

    # Claude extraction is sync (CPU-bound, not IO-bound under semaphore)
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    data = await loop.run_in_executor(
        None, _extract_with_llm, name, role, web_results, image_results
    )

    return data


async def scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=None) -> dict:
    """Scrape all pending judges. Handles seeding, rate limiting, and DB updates.

    Args:
        db: NeonHTTPClient instance
        on_progress: optional async callback(scraped, total, current_name)

    Returns:
        Summary dict with counts.
    """
    from judges.db import (
        seed_judges, get_pending_judges, update_judge_data, mark_judge_failed,
    )

    # Seed the full list (idempotent)
    await seed_judges(db, JUDGES_LIST)

    pending = await get_pending_judges(db)
    total = len(pending)
    if total == 0:
        return {"total": 0, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "message": "No pending judges"}

    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"total": total, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "error": "No SERPER_API_KEY set"}

    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
    scraped = 0
    failed = 0

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as http_client:
        # Process in batches of 3 with 1s delay between batches
        for i in range(0, total, 3):
            batch = pending[i:i + 3]
            tasks = []
            for judge in batch:
                tasks.append(
                    scrape_single_judge(
                        judge["name"], judge["role"],
                        http_client, api_key, semaphore,
                    )
                )
            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

            for judge, result in zip(batch, results):
                if isinstance(result, Exception):
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], str(result))
                    failed += 1
                elif result.get("confidence") == "none" or "error" in result:
                    error_msg = result.get("error", "Unknown extraction error")
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], error_msg)
                    failed += 1
                else:
                    # Store low-confidence note in scrape_error for review
                    if result.get("confidence") == "low":
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                        # Mark as scraped but note the low confidence
                        await db.execute(
                            "UPDATE judges SET scrape_error = $1 WHERE id = $2",
                            ["low confidence - may need manual review", judge["id"]],
                        )
                    else:
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                    scraped += 1

                if on_progress:
                    await on_progress(scraped + failed, total, judge["name"])

            # Rate limit: 1s between batches
            if i + 3 < total:
                await asyncio.sleep(1)

    return {"total": total, "scraped": scraped, "failed": failed}

"""Web scraper for judge/organizer biographical data and photos.

Uses Serper web search + image search to gather information,
then Claude Haiku to extract structured data from results.
"""

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os

import anthropic
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SERPER_SEARCH_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
SERPER_IMAGES_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/images"

# Full list of TreeHacks 2025 judges and organizers
JUDGES_LIST: list[dict] = [
    # Judges
    {"name": "Gautam Kumar", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Greg Feingold", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kyle Jeong", "role": "judge"},

    {"name": "Kshipra Dhame", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Prithvi Chaudhari", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ryan Tran", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jess Waterman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "David Yi", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Emma Kirst", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Claudia Dalmau", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Brandon", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Chad Rushing", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Daniel Sigman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Matt Luat", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Felicia Chang", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Connor Ling", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Maddie Bernheim", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Vincent Po", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Janelle Battad", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dj Isaac", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Max Forsey", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Zach Gulsby", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jeff Gardner", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Robin Lee", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sandy Orozco", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Hong Yi Chen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Lauren Goldberg", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Megan Ehrlich", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ojus Save", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Amanda Yiu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Advait Maybhate", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Martin Ceballos", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Marvin von Hagen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Michi Vinocour", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Samyok Nepal", "role": "judge"},
    # Organizers
    {"name": "Erin Shen", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Grace Wang", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Jerry Yao", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Madison Lea Ho", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Matthew Yu", "role": "organizer"},

    {"name": "Sahana Mantha", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Shrish Premkrishna", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Thryambak Ganapathy", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Toryn Thompson", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Tyler Rubenstein", "role": "organizer"},
]

# Names without a last name get extra context in the search query
_AMBIGUOUS_NAMES = {"Ben", "Brandon", "Dj Isaac"}


def _build_search_query(name: str, role: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper search query with appropriate context."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f'"{name}" TreeHacks 2025'
    role_label = "judge" if role == "judge" else "organizer"
    return f'"{name}" TreeHacks Stanford 2025 {role_label}'


def _build_image_query(name: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper image search query."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f"{name} TreeHacks headshot"
    return f"{name} headshot"


async def _serper_web_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper web search API and return organic results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_SEARCH_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("organic", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper web search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


async def _serper_image_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper image search API and return image results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_IMAGES_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("images", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper image search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


def _extract_with_llm(name: str, role: str, web_results: list[dict], image_results: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Use Claude Haiku to extract structured biographical data from search results.

    Returns a dict with: title, organization, bio, photo_url, linkedin_url,
    twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls.
    """
    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set"}

    # Format web results for the prompt
    web_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(web_results, 1):
        web_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Snippet: {r.get('snippet', '')}\n"
            f"   URL: {r.get('link', '')}\n\n"
        )

    # Format image results for the prompt
    image_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(image_results, 1):
        image_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Image URL: {r.get('imageUrl', '')}\n"
            f"   Source: {r.get('source', '')}\n\n"
        )

    prompt = (
        f"Extract biographical information about {name} (a TreeHacks 2025 {role}) "
        f"from these search results.\n\n"
        f"WEB RESULTS:\n{web_text}\n"
        f"IMAGE RESULTS:\n{image_text}\n"
        f"Return a JSON object with these exact keys:\n"
        f"- title: their job title or role (string or null)\n"
        f"- organization: their company or university (string or null)\n"
        f"- bio: a 2-3 sentence biography (string or null)\n"
        f"- photo_url: the best headshot URL from image results (string or null)\n"
        f"- linkedin_url: their LinkedIn profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- twitter_url: their Twitter/X profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- website_url: their personal website URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- confidence: how confident you are this data is correct - \"high\", \"medium\", or \"low\"\n"
        f"- source_urls: array of URLs where you found information\n\n"
        f"If the search results don't clearly match this person, set confidence to \"low\" "
        f"and leave fields as null. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
        message = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=512,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        )
        content = message.content[0].text
        return json.loads(content)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed for %s: %s", name, e)
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": str(e)}


async def scrape_single_judge(
    name: str,
    role: str,
    http_client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    api_key: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> dict:
    """Scrape biographical data for a single judge/organizer.

    Returns a dict with extracted data or an error.
    """
    async with semaphore:
        web_query = _build_search_query(name, role)
        image_query = _build_image_query(name)

        # Run web + image search in parallel
        web_results, image_results = await asyncio.gather(
            _serper_web_search(http_client, web_query, api_key),
            _serper_image_search(http_client, image_query, api_key),
        )

    # Claude extraction is sync (CPU-bound, not IO-bound under semaphore)
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    data = await loop.run_in_executor(
        None, _extract_with_llm, name, role, web_results, image_results
    )

    return data


async def scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=None) -> dict:
    """Scrape all pending judges. Handles seeding, rate limiting, and DB updates.

    Args:
        db: NeonHTTPClient instance
        on_progress: optional async callback(scraped, total, current_name)

    Returns:
        Summary dict with counts.
    """
    from judges.db import (
        seed_judges, get_pending_judges, update_judge_data, mark_judge_failed,
    )

    # Seed the full list (idempotent)
    await seed_judges(db, JUDGES_LIST)

    pending = await get_pending_judges(db)
    total = len(pending)
    if total == 0:
        return {"total": 0, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "message": "No pending judges"}

    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"total": total, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "error": "No SERPER_API_KEY set"}

    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
    scraped = 0
    failed = 0

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as http_client:
        # Process in batches of 3 with 1s delay between batches
        for i in range(0, total, 3):
            batch = pending[i:i + 3]
            tasks = []
            for judge in batch:
                tasks.append(
                    scrape_single_judge(
                        judge["name"], judge["role"],
                        http_client, api_key, semaphore,
                    )
                )
            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

            for judge, result in zip(batch, results):
                if isinstance(result, Exception):
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], str(result))
                    failed += 1
                elif result.get("confidence") == "none" or "error" in result:
                    error_msg = result.get("error", "Unknown extraction error")
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], error_msg)
                    failed += 1
                else:
                    # Store low-confidence note in scrape_error for review
                    if result.get("confidence") == "low":
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                        # Mark as scraped but note the low confidence
                        await db.execute(
                            "UPDATE judges SET scrape_error = $1 WHERE id = $2",
                            ["low confidence - may need manual review", judge["id"]],
                        )
                    else:
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                    scraped += 1

                if on_progress:
                    await on_progress(scraped + failed, total, judge["name"])

            # Rate limit: 1s between batches
            if i + 3 < total:
                await asyncio.sleep(1)

    return {"total": total, "scraped": scraped, "failed": failed}

"""FastAPI routes for the judges/organizers directory."""

from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel

from models.database import get_neon_client
from judges.db import get_all_judges, get_judge_by_id, manual_update_judge

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/judges", tags=["judges"])


class JudgeUpdateRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str | None = None
    role: str | None = None
    title: str | None = None
    organization: str | None = None
    bio: str | None = None
    photo_url: str | None = None
    linkedin_url: str | None = None
    twitter_url: str | None = None
    website_url: str | None = None


@router.get("/status")
async def scrape_status():
    """Get scrape progress counts."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        rows = await db.execute(
            "SELECT scrape_status, COUNT(*) as count FROM judges GROUP BY scrape_status"
        )
        counts = {r["scrape_status"]: int(r["count"]) for r in rows}
        total = sum(counts.values())
        return {
            "total": total,
            "pending": counts.get("pending", 0),
            "scraped": counts.get("scraped", 0),
            "failed": counts.get("failed", 0),
            "manual": counts.get("manual", 0),
        }
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("")
async def list_judges(role: str | None = None):
    """List all judges, optionally filtered by role."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        judges = await get_all_judges(db, role=role)
        return {"judges": judges, "count": len(judges)}
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("/{judge_id}")
async def get_judge(judge_id: str):
    """Get a single judge by UUID."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        judge = await get_judge_by_id(db, judge_id)
        if not judge:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Judge not found")
        return judge
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.put("/{judge_id}")
async def update_judge(judge_id: str, req: JudgeUpdateRequest):
    """Manually update/correct a judge record."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        existing = await get_judge_by_id(db, judge_id)
        if not existing:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Judge not found")
        updates = req.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
        if not updates:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No fields to update")
        await manual_update_judge(db, judge_id, updates)
        return {"status": "updated", "judge_id": judge_id}
    finally:
        await db.close()

from fastapi import FastAPI
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
import socketio

from routers import auth, queue, users
from scraper.routes import router as scraper_router
from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator

sio = socketio.AsyncServer(async_mode="asgi", cors_allowed_origins=[])

app = FastAPI(title="Mirrorless API", version="0.1.0")

app.include_router(auth.router)
app.include_router(queue.router)
app.include_router(users.router)
app.include_router(scraper_router)

# Make sio and Mira accessible to routes
app.state.sio = sio
mira = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=sio)
app.state.mira = mira


@app.get("/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}


# --- Socket.io events ---


@sio.event
async def connect(sid, environ):
    print(f"[socket] Client connected: {sid}")


@sio.event
async def join_room(sid, data):
    """Client joins a user-specific room for targeted events."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if user_id:
        await sio.enter_room(sid, user_id)
        print(f"[socket] {sid} joined room {user_id}")


@sio.event
async def disconnect(sid):
    print(f"[socket] Client disconnected: {sid}")


@sio.event
async def start_session(sid, data):
    """Start a Mira session for a user."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if not user_id:
        return
    print(f"[mira] Starting session for user {user_id}")
    await mira.start_session(user_id)


@sio.event
async def mirror_event(sid, data):
    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    event = data.get("event", {})
    if not user_id or not event:
        return
    await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)


@sio.event
async def end_session(sid, data):
    """End a Mira session."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if not user_id:
        return
    print(f"[mira] Ending session for user {user_id}")
    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
    if result:
        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)


# Wrap FastAPI with Socket.io, then wrap everything with CORS
_asgi_app = socketio.ASGIApp(sio, other_asgi_app=app)
socket_app = CORSMiddleware(
    _asgi_app,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

async def join_room(sid, data):
    """Client joins a user-specific room for targeted events."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if user_id:
        await sio.enter_room(sid, user_id)
        print(f"[socket] {sid} joined room {user_id}")


@sio.event
async def disconnect(sid):
    print(f"[socket] Client disconnected: {sid}")


@sio.event
async def start_session(sid, data):
    """Start a Mira session for a user."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if not user_id:
        return
    print(f"[mira] Starting session for user {user_id}")
    await mira.start_session(user_id)


@sio.event
async def mirror_event(sid, data):
    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    event = data.get("event", {})
    if not user_id or not event:
        return
    await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)


@sio.event
async def end_session(sid, data):
    """End a Mira session."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    if not user_id:
        return
    print(f"[mira] Ending session for user {user_id}")
    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
    if result:
        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)


# Wrap FastAPI with Socket.io, then wrap everything with CORS
_asgi_app = socketio.ASGIApp(sio, other_asgi_app=app)
socket_app = CORSMiddleware(
    _asgi_app,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

CREATE TABLE judges (
  id            uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name          text NOT NULL,
  role          text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'judge'
                  CHECK (role IN ('judge', 'organizer')),
  title         text,
  organization  text,
  bio           text,
  photo_url     text,
  linkedin_url  text,
  twitter_url   text,
  website_url   text,
  source_urls   jsonb DEFAULT '[]',
  scrape_status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                  CHECK (scrape_status IN ('pending', 'scraped', 'failed', 'manual')),
  scrape_error  text,
  scraped_at    timestamptz,
  created_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_judges_name_role ON judges(lower(name), role);
CREATE INDEX idx_judges_role ON judges(role);
CREATE INDEX idx_judges_scrape_status ON judges(scrape_status);

content.text:
All 61 judges seeded. Now let me launch the scraping blitz — 10 parallel agents at a time, each searching for a judge and returning structured JSON.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Instead of building a Serper→Claude pipeline (which costs API credits), I'm using my own WebSearch tool as the search engine and my own reasoning as the extractor. Each agent searches for a person, reads relevant pages, and returns structured biographical data. This is essentially free for you.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me launch the first batch of 10 judges.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Greg Feingold and his role in the Anthropic community:

Greg Feingold is currently serving in community programs at Anthropic since March 2025, having previously held the position of Community Program Manager at Perplexity from August 2024 to March 2025.

Greg Feingold leads the Builder Club program for Anthropic, and over 15,000 students have signed up. In a recent LinkedIn post, Greg noted that the program's momentum "has really exceeded our expectations since we started the program," with students building apps, agents, and MCP servers through the initiative each week.

His background reflects a strong focus on community engagement and ecosystem development within the augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) sectors. Before joining Anthropic, he contributed to TikTok's developer ecosystem as Global Community Lead, focusing on AR creation tools and developer SDKs, and led go-to-market efforts and cultivated developer relations for products such as Effect House, TikTok Open SDK, and Content Sharing API.

Rajesh Bhatia has 25+ years of experience in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and LLMs, and works at Apple in Apple Media Services. He is based in Mountain View and has a background from Stanford University.

His dissertation focused on "Analysis of Different Techniques of Improving Convergency of the Neural Networks Back-Propagation Algorithm" covering areas in Artificial Intelligence, Pattern Recognition, Machine Learning, and Artificial Neural Networks.

Beyond his role at Apple, Rajesh has completed multiple AI and machine learning certifications from Stanford University, including the AI Professional Certificate, XCS236 - Deep Generative Models, and XCS224N - NLP with Deep Learning.

His professional background also includes experience at other major tech companies, as he worked on data warehouse and Hive-Sparksql migration at Pinterest, and had a colleague relationship spanning 8 years at Sun Microsystems.

You Just Implemented the following plan:

# Plan: Judge Database & Web Scraper

## Context

We're building a judge/organizer directory for the Mirrorless hackathon project (TreeHacks). The goal is to scrape biographical data and a photo for ~50 judges and organizers from the web, store it in Neon Postgres, and expose it via REST endpoints. This data can be used on the mirror display or phone UI to show who's judging.

## New Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `backend/migrations/004_create_judges.sql` | Database migration |
| `backend/judges/__init__.py` | Module init |
| `backend/judges/db.py` | Database CRUD operations |
| `backend/judges/scraper.py` | Serper search + Claude extraction |
| `backend/judges/routes.py` | FastAPI endpoints + Pydantic models |

## Existing Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/main.py` | Add `from judges.routes import router as judges_router` and `app.include_router(judges_router)` |

## Step 1: Database Migration (`004_create_judges.sql`)

```sql
CREATE TABLE judges (
  id            uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name          text NOT NULL,
  role          text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'judge'
                  CHECK (role IN ('judge', 'organizer')),
  title         text,              -- "CEO", "Software Engineer", etc.
  organization  text,              -- "Google", "Stanford", etc.
  bio           text,              -- 2-3 sentence bio
  photo_url     text,              -- URL to best headshot found
  linkedin_url  text,
  twitter_url   text,
  website_url   text,
  source_urls   jsonb DEFAULT '[]',  -- URLs where data was found
  scrape_status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                  CHECK (scrape_status IN ('pending', 'scraped', 'failed', 'manual')),
  scrape_error  text,
  scraped_at    timestamptz,
  created_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_judges_name_role ON judges(lower(name), role);
CREATE INDEX idx_judges_role ON judges(role);
CREATE INDEX idx_judges_scrape_status ON judges(scrape_status);
```

## Step 2: Database Operations (`judges/db.py`)

Follow the pattern from `backend/scraper/db.py` — each function takes a `db` (NeonHTTPClient) parameter with `$1, $2` parameterized queries.

Functions:
- **`seed_judges(db, judges_list)`** — Bulk insert names with `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (idempotent)
- **`get_pending_judges(db)`** — Fetch judges with `scrape_status = 'pending'`
- **`update_judge_data(db, judge_id, data)`** — Update with scraped data, set status to `'scraped'`
- **`mark_judge_failed(db, judge_id, error)`** — Set status to `'failed'` with error message
- **`get_all_judges(db, role=None)`** — List all, optionally filtered by role
- **`get_judge_by_id(db, judge_id)`** — Single lookup by UUID
- **`manual_update_judge(db, judge_id, updates)`** — For PUT endpoint, sets status to `'manual'`

## Step 3: Web Scraper (`judges/scraper.py`)

### Hardcoded Judge List
The full list of 50+ names with their roles embedded as a constant `JUDGES_LIST`.

### Scraping Pipeline (per judge)

1. **Serper Web Search** → `POST https://google.serper.dev/search`
   - Query: `"{name}" TreeHacks Stanford 2025 judge` (or `organizer`)
   - For ambiguous names ("Ben", "Brandon"): `"{name}" TreeHacks 2025`
   - Returns top 5 organic results (title, snippet, link)

2. **Serper Image Search** → `POST https://google.serper.dev/images`
   - Query: `"{name}" TreeHacks headshot`
   - Returns top 5 image results (imageUrl, title)

3. **Claude Haiku Extraction** — same pattern as `purchase_parser.py:_extract_with_llm`
   - Feed web + image results into a prompt
   - Ask for JSON: `{title, organization, bio, best_photo_url, linkedin_url, twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls}`
   - `max_tokens=512`, model `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`

### Rate Limiting
- `asyncio.Semaphore(2)` for concurrent Serper calls
- 1-second sleep between batches of 3 judges
- ~100 Serper calls total (well within 2,500/month free tier)
- ~$0.02 total Claude API cost

### Ambiguous Name Handling
Names with no last name ("Ben", "Brandon", "Dj Isaac") get:
- "TreeHacks" appended to the name as context (e.g., `"Ben TreeHacks"`)
- Same search pipeline as other judges, just with this extra qualifier
- Claude returns `confidence: "low"` if results are still unclear
- Low-confidence entries stored with note in `scrape_error` for manual review

## Step 4: API Routes (`judges/routes.py`)

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| POST | `/judges/scrape` | Trigger scraping (background task via `asyncio.create_task`) |
| GET | `/judges/status` | Scrape progress: `{total, pending, scraped, failed}` |
| GET | `/judges` | List all judges (optional `?role=judge\|organizer` filter) |
| GET | `/judges/{judge_id}` | Single judge by UUID |
| PUT | `/judges/{judge_id}` | Manual update/correction |

**Note:** `/judges/status` must be defined before `/judges/{judge_id}` in the router to avoid FastAPI treating "status" as a UUID.

### Pydantic Models (inline in routes.py, same pattern as `scraper/routes.py`)
- `ScrapeJudgesRequest` — optional `names: list[str]`, `retry_failed: bool`
- `JudgeUpdateRequest` — all optional fields for manual edits
- `JudgeResponse` — full judge record

## Step 5: Register Router in `main.py`

Add two lines to `backend/main.py`:
```python
from judges.routes import router as judges_router
app.include_router(judges_router)
```

## Step 6: Run Migration

Execute `004_create_judges.sql` against Neon via the Neon MCP `run_sql` tool.

## Implementation Order

1. Migration file → run it
2. `judges/__init__.py`
3. `judges/db.py`
4. `judges/scraper.py` (largest file, ~200 lines)
5. `judges/routes.py`
6. `main.py` router registration
7. Test: `POST /judges/scrape` → poll `GET /judges/status` → `GET /judges`

## Verification

1. Run the migration against Neon
2. Start the backend: `uvicorn main:app --reload`
3. `POST /judges/scrape` — triggers background scraping
4. `GET /judges/status` — monitor progress
5. `GET /judges` — verify scraped data and photos
6. `GET /judges?role=organizer` — verify role filtering
7. `PUT /judges/{id}` — test manual correction for low-confidence entries

   129→        resp = await client.post(
   130→            SERPER_IMAGES_URL,
   131→            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
   132→            json={"q": query, "num": num},
   133→        )
   134→        resp.raise_for_status()
   135→        data = resp.json()
   136→        return data.get("images", [])[:num]
   137→    except Exception as e:
   138→        logger.warning("Serper image search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
   139→        return []

   142→def _extract_with_llm(name: str, role: str, web_results: list[dict], image_results: list[dict]) -> dict:
   143→    """Use Claude Haiku to extract structured biographical data from search results.
   144→
   145→    Returns a dict with: title, organization, bio, photo_url, linkedin_url,
   146→    twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls.
   147→    """
   148→    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   149→    if not api_key:
   150→        return {"confidence": "none", "error": "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set"}
   151→
   152→    # Format web results for the prompt
   153→    web_text = ""
   154→    for i, r in enumerate(web_results, 1):
   155→        web_text += (
   156→            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
   157→            f"   Snippet: {r.get('snippet', '')}\n"
   158→            f"   URL: {r.get('link', '')}\n\n"
   159→        )
   160→
   161→    # Format image results for the prompt
   162→    image_text = ""
   163→    for i, r in enumerate(image_results, 1):
   164→        image_text += (
   165→            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
   166→            f"   Image URL: {r.get('imageUrl', '')}\n"
   167→            f"   Source: {r.get('source', '')}\n\n"
   168→        )
   169→
   170→    prompt = (
   171→        f"Extract biographical information about {name} (a TreeHacks 2025 {role}) "
   172→        f"from these search results.\n\n"
   173→        f"WEB RESULTS:\n{web_text}\n"
   174→        f"IMAGE RESULTS:\n{image_text}\n"
   175→        f"Return a JSON object with these exact keys:\n"
   176→        f"- title: their job title or role (string or null)\n"
   177→        f"- organization: their company or university (string or null)\n"
   178→        f"- bio: a 2-3 sentence biography (string or null)\n"
   179→        f"- photo_url: the best headshot URL from image results (string or null)\n"
   180→        f"- linkedin_url: their LinkedIn profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
   181→        f"- twitter_url: their Twitter/X profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
   182→        f"- website_url: their personal website URL if found (string or null)\n"
   183→        f"- confidence: how confident you are this data is correct - \"high\", \"medium\", or \"low\"\n"
   184→        f"- source_urls: array of URLs where you found information\n\n"
   185→        f"If the search results don't clearly match this person, set confidence to \"low\" "
   186→        f"and leave fields as null. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."
   187→    )
   188→
   189→    try:
   190→        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
   191→        message = client.messages.create(
   192→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   193→            max_tokens=512,
   194→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
   195→        )
   196→        content = message.content[0].text
   197→        return json.loads(content)
   198→    except Exception as e:
   199→        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed for %s: %s", name, e)
   200→        return {"confidence": "none", "error": str(e)}
   201→
   202→
   203→async def scrape_single_judge(
   204→    name: str,
   205→    role: str,
   206→    http_client: httpx.AsyncClient,
   207→    api_key: str,
   208→    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
   209→) -> dict:
   210→    """Scrape biographical data for a single judge/organizer.
   211→
   212→    Returns a dict with extracted data or an error.
   213→    """
   214→    async with semaphore:
   215→        web_query = _build_search_query(name, role)
   216→        image_query = _build_image_query(name)
   217→
   218→        # Run web + image search in parallel
   219→        web_results, image_results = await asyncio.gather(
   220→            _serper_web_search(http_client, web_query, api_key),
   221→            _serper_image_search(http_client, image_query, api_key),
   222→        )
   223→
   224→    # Claude extraction is sync (CPU-bound, not IO-bound under semaphore)
   225→    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   226→    data = await loop.run_in_executor(
   227→        None, _extract_with_llm, name, role, web_results, image_results
   228→    )
   229→
   230→    return data
   231→
   232→
   233→async def scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=None) -> dict:
   234→    """Scrape all pending judges. Handles seeding, rate limiting, and DB updates.
   235→
   236→    Args:
   237→        db: NeonHTTPClient instance
   238→        on_progress: optional async callback(scraped, total, current_name)
   239→
   240→    Returns:
   241→        Summary dict with counts.
   242→    """
   243→    from judges.db import (
   244→        seed_judges, get_pending_judges, update_judge_data, mark_judge_failed,
   245→    )
   246→
   247→    # Seed the full list (idempotent)
   248→    await seed_judges(db, JUDGES_LIST)
   249→
   250→    pending = await get_pending_judges(db)
   251→    total = len(pending)
   252→    if total == 0:
   253→        return {"total": 0, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "message": "No pending judges"}
   254→
   255→    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
   256→    if not api_key:
   257→        return {"total": total, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "error": "No SERPER_API_KEY set"}
   258→
   259→    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
   260→    scraped = 0
   261→    failed = 0
   262→
   263→    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as http_client:
   264→        # Process in batches of 3 with 1s delay between batches
   265→        for i in range(0, total, 3):
   266→            batch = pending[i:i + 3]
   267→            tasks = []
   268→            for judge in batch:
   269→                tasks.append(
   270→                    scrape_single_judge(
   271→                        judge["name"], judge["role"],
   272→                        http_client, api_key, semaphore,
   273→                    )
   274→                )
   275→            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
   276→
   277→            for judge, result in zip(batch, results):
   278→                if isinstance(result, Exception):
   279→                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], str(result))
   280→                    failed += 1
   281→                elif result.get("confidence") == "none" or "error" in result:
   282→                    error_msg = result.get("error", "Unknown extraction error")
   283→                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], error_msg)
   284→                    failed += 1
   285→                else:
   286→                    # Store low-confidence note in scrape_error for review
   287→                    if result.get("confidence") == "low":
   288→                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
   289→                            "title": result.get("title"),
   290→                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
   291→                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
   292→                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
   293→                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
   294→                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
   295→                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
   296→                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
   297→                        })
   298→                        # Mark as scraped but note the low confidence
   299→                        await db.execute(
   300→                            "UPDATE judges SET scrape_error = $1 WHERE id = $2",
   301→                            ["low confidence - may need manual review", judge["id"]],

"""Web scraper for judge/organizer biographical data and photos.

Uses Serper web search + image search to gather information,
then Claude Haiku to extract structured data from results.
"""

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os

import anthropic
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SERPER_SEARCH_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
SERPER_IMAGES_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/images"

# Full list of TreeHacks 2025 judges and organizers
JUDGES_LIST: list[dict] = [
    # Judges
    {"name": "Gautam Kumar", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Greg Feingold", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kyle Jeong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Shrey Pandya", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Diyi Zhu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sarah Josief", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Craig Dennis", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Judy Cheong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Rajesh Bhatia", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sabrina Farmin", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ben", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kelsea An", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Olivia Petrie", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sana Wajid", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Abhi Gangani", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dev Chauhan", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kshipra Dhame", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Prithvi Chaudhari", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ryan Tran", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jess Waterman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "David Yi", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Emma Kirst", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Claudia Dalmau", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Brandon", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Chad Rushing", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Daniel Sigman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Matt Luat", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Felicia Chang", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Connor Ling", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Maddie Bernheim", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Vincent Po", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Janelle Battad", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dj Isaac", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Max Forsey", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Zach Gulsby", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jeff Gardner", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Robin Lee", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sandy Orozco", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Hong Yi Chen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Lauren Goldberg", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Megan Ehrlich", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ojus Save", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Amanda Yiu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Advait Maybhate", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Martin Ceballos", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Marvin von Hagen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Michi Vinocour", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Samyok Nepal", "role": "judge"},
    # Organizers
    {"name": "Erin Shen", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Grace Wang", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Jerry Yao", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Madison Lea Ho", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Matthew Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Maxwell Spivakovsky", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Michael Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Oliver Sin", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Sahana Mantha", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Shrish Premkrishna", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Thryambak Ganapathy", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Toryn Thompson", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Tyler Rubenstein", "role": "organizer"},
]

# Names without a last name get extra context in the search query
_AMBIGUOUS_NAMES = {"Ben", "Brandon", "Dj Isaac"}


def _build_search_query(name: str, role: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper search query with appropriate context."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f'"{name}" TreeHacks 2025'
    role_label = "judge" if role == "judge" else "organizer"
    return f'"{name}" TreeHacks Stanford 2025 {role_label}'


def _build_image_query(name: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper image search query."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f"{name} TreeHacks headshot"
    return f"{name} headshot"


async def _serper_web_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper web search API and return organic results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_SEARCH_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("organic", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper web search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


async def _serper_image_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper image search API and return image results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_IMAGES_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("images", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper image search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


def _extract_with_llm(name: str, role: str, web_results: list[dict], image_results: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Use Claude Haiku to extract structured biographical data from search results.

    Returns a dict with: title, organization, bio, photo_url, linkedin_url,
    twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls.
    """
    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set"}

    # Format web results for the prompt
    web_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(web_results, 1):
        web_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Snippet: {r.get('snippet', '')}\n"
            f"   URL: {r.get('link', '')}\n\n"
        )

    # Format image results for the prompt
    image_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(image_results, 1):
        image_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Image URL: {r.get('imageUrl', '')}\n"
            f"   Source: {r.get('source', '')}\n\n"
        )

    prompt = (
        f"Extract biographical information about {name} (a TreeHacks 2025 {role}) "
        f"from these search results.\n\n"
        f"WEB RESULTS:\n{web_text}\n"
        f"IMAGE RESULTS:\n{image_text}\n"
        f"Return a JSON object with these exact keys:\n"
        f"- title: their job title or role (string or null)\n"
        f"- organization: their company or university (string or null)\n"
        f"- bio: a 2-3 sentence biography (string or null)\n"
        f"- photo_url: the best headshot URL from image results (string or null)\n"
        f"- linkedin_url: their LinkedIn profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- twitter_url: their Twitter/X profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- website_url: their personal website URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- confidence: how confident you are this data is correct - \"high\", \"medium\", or \"low\"\n"
        f"- source_urls: array of URLs where you found information\n\n"
        f"If the search results don't clearly match this person, set confidence to \"low\" "
        f"and leave fields as null. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
        message = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=512,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        )
        content = message.content[0].text
        return json.loads(content)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed for %s: %s", name, e)
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": str(e)}


async def scrape_single_judge(
    name: str,
    role: str,
    http_client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    api_key: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> dict:
    """Scrape biographical data for a single judge/organizer.

    Returns a dict with extracted data or an error.
    """
    async with semaphore:
        web_query = _build_search_query(name, role)
        image_query = _build_image_query(name)

        # Run web + image search in parallel
        web_results, image_results = await asyncio.gather(
            _serper_web_search(http_client, web_query, api_key),
            _serper_image_search(http_client, image_query, api_key),
        )

    # Claude extraction is sync (CPU-bound, not IO-bound under semaphore)
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    data = await loop.run_in_executor(
        None, _extract_with_llm, name, role, web_results, image_results
    )

    return data


async def scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=None) -> dict:
    """Scrape all pending judges. Handles seeding, rate limiting, and DB updates.

    Args:
        db: NeonHTTPClient instance
        on_progress: optional async callback(scraped, total, current_name)

    Returns:
        Summary dict with counts.
    """
    from judges.db import (
        seed_judges, get_pending_judges, update_judge_data, mark_judge_failed,
    )

    # Seed the full list (idempotent)
    await seed_judges(db, JUDGES_LIST)

    pending = await get_pending_judges(db)
    total = len(pending)
    if total == 0:
        return {"total": 0, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "message": "No pending judges"}

    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"total": total, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "error": "No SERPER_API_KEY set"}

    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
    scraped = 0
    failed = 0

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as http_client:
        # Process in batches of 3 with 1s delay between batches
        for i in range(0, total, 3):
            batch = pending[i:i + 3]
            tasks = []
            for judge in batch:
                tasks.append(
                    scrape_single_judge(
                        judge["name"], judge["role"],
                        http_client, api_key, semaphore,
                    )
                )
            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

            for judge, result in zip(batch, results):
                if isinstance(result, Exception):
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], str(result))
                    failed += 1
                elif result.get("confidence") == "none" or "error" in result:
                    error_msg = result.get("error", "Unknown extraction error")
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], error_msg)
                    failed += 1
                else:
                    # Store low-confidence note in scrape_error for review
                    if result.get("confidence") == "low":
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                        # Mark as scraped but note the low confidence
                        await db.execute(
                            "UPDATE judges SET scrape_error = $1 WHERE id = $2",
                            ["low confidence - may need manual review", judge["id"]],
                        )
                    else:
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                    scraped += 1

                if on_progress:
                    await on_progress(scraped + failed, total, judge["name"])

            # Rate limit: 1s between batches
            if i + 3 < total:
                await asyncio.sleep(1)

    return {"total": total, "scraped": scraped, "failed": failed}

"""FastAPI routes for the judges/organizers directory."""

from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel

from models.database import get_neon_client
from judges.db import get_all_judges, get_judge_by_id, manual_update_judge

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/judges", tags=["judges"])


class JudgeUpdateRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str | None = None
    role: str | None = None
    title: str | None = None
    organization: str | None = None
    bio: str | None = None
    photo_url: str | None = None
    linkedin_url: str | None = None
    twitter_url: str | None = None
    website_url: str | None = None


@router.get("/status")
async def scrape_status():
    """Get scrape progress counts."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        rows = await db.execute(
            "SELECT scrape_status, COUNT(*) as count FROM judges GROUP BY scrape_status"
        )
        counts = {r["scrape_status"]: int(r["count"]) for r in rows}
        total = sum(counts.values())
        return {
            "total": total,
            "pending": counts.get("pending", 0),
            "scraped": counts.get("scraped", 0),
            "failed": counts.get("failed", 0),
            "manual": counts.get("manual", 0),
        }
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("")
async def list_judges(role: str | None = None):
    """List all judges, optionally filtered by role."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        judges = await get_all_judges(db, role=role)
        return {"judges": judges, "count": len(judges)}
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("/{judge_id}")
async def get_judge(judge_id: str):
    """Get a single judge by UUID."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        judge = await get_judge_by_id(db, judge_id)
        if not judge:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Judge not found")
        return judge
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.put("/{judge_id}")
async def update_judge(judge_id: str, req: JudgeUpdateRequest):
    """Manually update/correct a judge record."""
    db = await get_neon_client()
    try:
        existing = await get_judge_by_id(db, judge_id)
        if not existing:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Judge not found")
        updates = req.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
        if not updates:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No fields to update")
        await manual_update_judge(db, judge_id, updates)
        return {"status": "updated", "judge_id": judge_id}
    finally:
        await db.close()

async def run_scrape():
    from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    from judges.scraper import scrape_all_judges

    try:
        async def progress(done, total, name):
            print(f'  [{done}/{total}] Just processed: {name}', flush=True)

        print('Starting judge scraping...', flush=True)
        result = await scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=progress)
        print(f'\\nDone! Result: {result}', flush=True)
    finally:
        await db.close()

asyncio.run(run_scrape())

I can see the scraper is running but there's a major problem — every single Claude Haiku extraction is failing with `Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)`. This is a JSON parsing error, meaning Claude is returning something that can't be parsed as JSON. 

The issue is likely that Claude Haiku is wrapping the JSON in markdown code fences (```json ... ```) even though the prompt says "Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences." Let me check the `_extract_with_llm` function more carefully.

Looking at lines 196-197:
```python
content = message.content[0].text


return json.loads(content)
```

The problem is Claude is likely returning the JSON wrapped in markdown fences like:
```json
{...}
```

And the `json.loads` call fails because it can't parse the markdown fences. All judges are being marked as "failed" because of this.

I need to strip the markdown fences from the response before parsing it as JSON, then stop the running scraper to avoid wasting API credits, reset the failed judges back to pending, and re-run the extraction.

import asyncio
import os
import sys

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, stream=sys.stdout, format='%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s')

async def run_scrape():
    from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    from judges.scraper import scrape_all_judges

    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    try:
        async def progress(done, total, name):
            print(f'  [{done}/{total}] Just processed: {name}', flush=True)

        await db.close()

asyncio.run(run_scrape())

"""Web scraper for judge/organizer biographical data and photos.

Uses Serper web search + image search to gather information,
then Claude Haiku to extract structured data from results.
"""

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os

import anthropic
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SERPER_SEARCH_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
SERPER_IMAGES_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/images"

# Full list of TreeHacks 2025 judges and organizers
JUDGES_LIST: list[dict] = [
    # Judges
    {"name": "Gautam Kumar", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Greg Feingold", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kyle Jeong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Shrey Pandya", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Diyi Zhu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sarah Josief", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Craig Dennis", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Judy Cheong", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Rajesh Bhatia", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sabrina Farmin", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ben", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kelsea An", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Olivia Petrie", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sana Wajid", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Abhi Gangani", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dev Chauhan", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Kshipra Dhame", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Prithvi Chaudhari", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ryan Tran", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jess Waterman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "David Yi", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Emma Kirst", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Claudia Dalmau", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Brandon", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Chad Rushing", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Daniel Sigman", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Matt Luat", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Felicia Chang", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Connor Ling", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Maddie Bernheim", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Vincent Po", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Janelle Battad", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Dj Isaac", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Max Forsey", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Zach Gulsby", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Jeff Gardner", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Robin Lee", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Sandy Orozco", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Hong Yi Chen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Lauren Goldberg", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Megan Ehrlich", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Ojus Save", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Amanda Yiu", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Advait Maybhate", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Martin Ceballos", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Marvin von Hagen", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Michi Vinocour", "role": "judge"},
    {"name": "Samyok Nepal", "role": "judge"},
    # Organizers
    {"name": "Erin Shen", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Grace Wang", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Jerry Yao", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Madison Lea Ho", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Matthew Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Maxwell Spivakovsky", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Michael Yu", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Oliver Sin", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Sahana Mantha", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Shrish Premkrishna", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Thryambak Ganapathy", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Toryn Thompson", "role": "organizer"},
    {"name": "Tyler Rubenstein", "role": "organizer"},
]

# Names without a last name get extra context in the search query
_AMBIGUOUS_NAMES = {"Ben", "Brandon", "Dj Isaac"}


def _build_search_query(name: str, role: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper search query with appropriate context."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f'"{name}" TreeHacks 2025'
    role_label = "judge" if role == "judge" else "organizer"
    return f'"{name}" TreeHacks Stanford 2025 {role_label}'


def _build_image_query(name: str) -> str:
    """Build a Serper image search query."""
    if name in _AMBIGUOUS_NAMES:
        return f"{name} TreeHacks headshot"
    return f"{name} headshot"


async def _serper_web_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper web search API and return organic results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_SEARCH_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("organic", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper web search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


async def _serper_image_search(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, query: str, api_key: str, num: int = 5
) -> list[dict]:
    """Call Serper image search API and return image results."""
    try:
        resp = await client.post(
            SERPER_IMAGES_URL,
            headers={"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
            json={"q": query, "num": num},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        return data.get("images", [])[:num]
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Serper image search failed for %r: %s", query, e)
        return []


def _extract_with_llm(name: str, role: str, web_results: list[dict], image_results: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Use Claude Haiku to extract structured biographical data from search results.

    Returns a dict with: title, organization, bio, photo_url, linkedin_url,
    twitter_url, website_url, confidence, source_urls.
    """
    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set"}

    # Format web results for the prompt
    web_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(web_results, 1):
        web_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Snippet: {r.get('snippet', '')}\n"
            f"   URL: {r.get('link', '')}\n\n"
        )

    # Format image results for the prompt
    image_text = ""
    for i, r in enumerate(image_results, 1):
        image_text += (
            f"{i}. Title: {r.get('title', '')}\n"
            f"   Image URL: {r.get('imageUrl', '')}\n"
            f"   Source: {r.get('source', '')}\n\n"
        )

    prompt = (
        f"Extract biographical information about {name} (a TreeHacks 2025 {role}) "
        f"from these search results.\n\n"
        f"WEB RESULTS:\n{web_text}\n"
        f"IMAGE RESULTS:\n{image_text}\n"
        f"Return a JSON object with these exact keys:\n"
        f"- title: their job title or role (string or null)\n"
        f"- organization: their company or university (string or null)\n"
        f"- bio: a 2-3 sentence biography (string or null)\n"
        f"- photo_url: the best headshot URL from image results (string or null)\n"
        f"- linkedin_url: their LinkedIn profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- twitter_url: their Twitter/X profile URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- website_url: their personal website URL if found (string or null)\n"
        f"- confidence: how confident you are this data is correct - \"high\", \"medium\", or \"low\"\n"
        f"- source_urls: array of URLs where you found information\n\n"
        f"If the search results don't clearly match this person, set confidence to \"low\" "
        f"and leave fields as null. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."
    )

    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
        message = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=512,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        )
        content = message.content[0].text
        return json.loads(content)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed for %s: %s", name, e)
        return {"confidence": "none", "error": str(e)}


async def scrape_single_judge(
    name: str,
    role: str,
    http_client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    api_key: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> dict:
    """Scrape biographical data for a single judge/organizer.

    Returns a dict with extracted data or an error.
    """
    async with semaphore:
        web_query = _build_search_query(name, role)
        image_query = _build_image_query(name)

        # Run web + image search in parallel
        web_results, image_results = await asyncio.gather(
            _serper_web_search(http_client, web_query, api_key),
            _serper_image_search(http_client, image_query, api_key),
        )

    # Claude extraction is sync (CPU-bound, not IO-bound under semaphore)
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    data = await loop.run_in_executor(
        None, _extract_with_llm, name, role, web_results, image_results
    )

    return data


async def scrape_all_judges(db, on_progress=None) -> dict:
    """Scrape all pending judges. Handles seeding, rate limiting, and DB updates.

    Args:
        db: NeonHTTPClient instance
        on_progress: optional async callback(scraped, total, current_name)

    Returns:
        Summary dict with counts.
    """
    from judges.db import (
        seed_judges, get_pending_judges, update_judge_data, mark_judge_failed,
    )

    # Seed the full list (idempotent)
    await seed_judges(db, JUDGES_LIST)

    pending = await get_pending_judges(db)
    total = len(pending)
    if total == 0:
        return {"total": 0, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "message": "No pending judges"}

    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        return {"total": total, "scraped": 0, "failed": 0, "error": "No SERPER_API_KEY set"}

    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
    scraped = 0
    failed = 0

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as http_client:
        # Process in batches of 3 with 1s delay between batches
        for i in range(0, total, 3):
            batch = pending[i:i + 3]
            tasks = []
            for judge in batch:
                tasks.append(
                    scrape_single_judge(
                        judge["name"], judge["role"],
                        http_client, api_key, semaphore,
                    )
                )
            results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

            for judge, result in zip(batch, results):
                if isinstance(result, Exception):
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], str(result))
                    failed += 1
                elif result.get("confidence") == "none" or "error" in result:
                    error_msg = result.get("error", "Unknown extraction error")
                    await mark_judge_failed(db, judge["id"], error_msg)
                    failed += 1
                else:
                    # Store low-confidence note in scrape_error for review
                    if result.get("confidence") == "low":
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                        # Mark as scraped but note the low confidence
                        await db.execute(
                            "UPDATE judges SET scrape_error = $1 WHERE id = $2",
                            ["low confidence - may need manual review", judge["id"]],
                        )
                    else:
                        await update_judge_data(db, judge["id"], {
                            "title": result.get("title"),
                            "organization": result.get("organization"),
                            "bio": result.get("bio"),
                            "photo_url": result.get("photo_url"),
                            "linkedin_url": result.get("linkedin_url"),
                            "twitter_url": result.get("twitter_url"),
                            "website_url": result.get("website_url"),
                            "source_urls": result.get("source_urls", []),
                        })
                    scraped += 1

                if on_progress:
                    await on_progress(scraped + failed, total, judge["name"])

            # Rate limit: 1s between batches
            if i + 3 < total:
                await asyncio.sleep(1)

    return {"total": total, "scraped": scraped, "failed": failed}
